meaning: n.派别活动,派性,党派主义,党派之争
examples: Resentment against Germanic chieftains achieving high rank in the Roman military and factionalism among Roman leaders were among the causes of the period's considerable instability.
Will they oppose factionalism or not?
Second, it is imperative to struggle against factionalism.
In other industrialized democracies this divide is more muddled by regional interests or other factionalism.
Further north, in Jenin, the Palestinian movement is rent by the same violent factionalism.
